pi_items.source: Task description (task.json line 6), setup_task.sh (line 43), export_result.sh (line 47), verifier.py (line 42) - all consistently reference creating a plan named 'Quarterly'
Task description specifies '3 Months', export_result.sh (line 54) checks for plan['number_of_months'] == 3, verifier.py (line 42) mentions '3 months'

Task description explicitly names the opportunity, setup_task.sh (line 35) searches for leads with this exact name, export_result.sh (line 64) searches for this name, verifier.py (line 52) confirms this is the expected opportunity

Task description specifies 'Expected Revenue (One-time): 2,500', export_result.sh (line 73) validates this value with floating point comparison to 2500.0, verifier.py (line 62) confirms '2500' as expected
Task description specifies 'Recurring Revenue: 600', export_result.sh (line 74) validates with comparison to 600.0, verifier.py (line 69) confirms '600' as expected

privileged_info_summary: The task is to configure Odoo 17 CRM (real enterprise software, version 17.0 running in Docker) to enable the Recurring Revenues feature and create specific test data. All five metadata values are verified: the plan name is 'Quarterly' with 3-month duration, the opportunity name is 'Apex Logic - Enterprise Bundle' with one-time revenue of 2500.0 and recurring revenue of 600.0. These are synthetic test values created by the agent as part of the task execution (not pre-seeded in the environment). The task uses real Odoo 17 CRM APIs (XML-RPC) and validates data against actual Odoo models (crm.recurring.plan and crm.lead) with proper field names (number_of_months, expected_revenue, recurring_revenue, recurring_plan_id).
